axis: omega-audio-extended-1 (parallel zero injection — notch organ for nasal antiresonance)

organ_added.name: notch_hexa(in_path: string, notch_hz: float, width_hz: float) -> string
organ_added.pattern_followed: ω-audio-6 redo: array_alloc(44 + 2*n) + indexed-write byte buffer (interp-4 backed). Mirrors pipe_hexa structure exactly: read input WAV via _read_wav_int16_le → per-sample biquad → WAV header + LE int16 write.

algorithm.filter_type: RBJ direct-form-I biquad notch (Audio EQ Cookbook)
algorithm.zero_locations_z_plane: complex conjugate pair on unit circle at z = e^±j·omega — true transmission zero
algorithm.pole_locations_z_plane: complex conjugate pair just inside unit circle at radius ≈ 1 - alpha

coefficient_formulas.q_form_chosen: Q = notch_hz / width_hz (bandwidth-in-Hz form; mirrors breath_hexa lines 643-645 convention)

deviation_from_spec.spec_alpha: alpha = sin(omega) · sinh(ln(2)/2 · width · omega/sin(omega))   (RBJ BW-in-octaves form)
deviation_from_spec.implemented_alpha: alpha = sin(omega) / (2·Q),  Q = notch_hz / width_hz   (RBJ Q-from-Hz-bandwidth form)
deviation_from_spec.rationale: Spec wrote width parameter in Hz units but quoted the BW-in-octaves alpha formula — dimensionally inconsistent. The Q-from-Hz form yields the same canonical RBJ notch for the same physical bandwidth and avoids dependence on sinh / ln (not confirmed available in the hexa stdlib used by anima_audio.hexa). breath_hexa (line 644) already follows this Q = f0/BW convention.
deviation_from_spec.result_equivalence: identical zero/pole geometry for matching physical bandwidth; depth target met with width=200Hz (Q=5).

algorithm.operation_count_per_sample: 5 mul + 4 add + 4 register shifts (x1,x2,y1,y2 update) → ~50 µs/sample worst case in interpreted hexa.

public_dispatch.fallback: no python heredoc — both branches route to notch_hexa. Per session: hexa-only synthesis. The env-gated structure is preserved (mirrors vocal/breath/pipe/vocal_lf dispatch pattern) so a future python implementation can be slotted in without changing call sites.

omega_b_validation.validator: /tmp/omega_audio_7_analyze.py (FFT analyzer, python — analyzer-only, NOT in synth path)
omega_b_validation.fft_setup: Hann window, np.fft.rfft over full 300ms (7200 samples @ 24 kHz)

w100_Q10.verdict: FAIL (notch too narrow — averages over band dilute the bin-center null)

headline_result.structural_advance: depth no longer arises from harmonic spacing of the 120 Hz source — it is now produced by a true z-plane zero pair on the unit circle. Robust to F0 / Q changes, unlike the all-pole pipe-only notch.

notes: w200 (Q=5) gives the best avg-band depth because it hits the sweet spot between (a) deep enough null at the bin center and (b) wide enough notch to dominate the band-average metric.
Narrower notches (w50/w100) produce a deeper point null (min_in_band ≈ -100 dB) but cover too few FFT bins to lower the band average.
anima can vary width to discover /m/ (deep narrow), /n/ (deeper still), /ŋ/ (broad), /l/ (lateral) without any dictionary.

bench.verdict: MARGINAL (276 vs 250 target — 10% over; first run included in mean for reproducibility, steady-state runs 4-5 hit 273 ms)
bench.interpretation: Per-sample interpreted-hexa biquad pass dominates: 2400 samples × ~50 µs = 120 ms compute, plus _read_wav_int16_le LE-decode loop (~80 ms for 2400 samples) and array_alloc + indexed write (~70 ms). Within an order of magnitude of the spec estimate (~240 ms compute + IO). Future improvement: SIMD-friendly inner loop or native intrinsic for the biquad pass would put it well below 100 ms.

comparison.notch_hexa_overhead_factor: ~7x pipe_hexa — biquad cost similar but read+write IO is the same; the bottleneck is the per-sample interpreted loop, identical structurally to pipe_hexa.

files_NOT_touched: git working tree commit (no commit per directive)

next_cycle_recommendation.primary: omega-audio-extended-4 (LPC formant estimator) — orthogonal axis closing Ω-E (2/8 → ≥7/8 Korean monophthong coverage). Measurement-side, anima_audio.hexa untouched.

secondary_apply_biquad_to_other_organs.rationale: RBJ biquad is general — same 2-zero/2-pole engine could host: low_shelf, high_shelf (already present in older non-hexa-native paths), peaking EQ, allpass. A single hexa-native _biquad_pass() helper would let anima place arbitrary spectral features without per-organ rewrites.
secondary_apply_biquad_to_other_organs.philosophy_compliance: PASS — additive organs, no training, no dictionary

next_cycle_recommendation.deferred: notch as parallel-branch (subtractive) for anatomically-motivated nasal coupling — current cascade-zero is the modular minimum; parallel branch is an exploration axis only if anima discovers it needs sub-band amplitude control beyond a notch.
